topic: Nuclear fusion commercialization outlook and strategic implications
objective: Assess the commercial viability and strategic implications of nuclear fusion energy, focusing on timeline, cost competitiveness, and market entry scenarios.
decision_question: Should our organization invest in or prepare for nuclear fusion as a commercially viable energy source within the next 15 years?

high_confidence_facts: [Source 14: DOE FY 2027 Fusion Energy Sciences budget request] 406 million below FY 2026 Enacted, with reduced funding for core research to offset increases for high priority research activities and facility operations.

[Source 1: U.S. DOE Fusion Energy] The Fusion Science & Technology (S&T) Roadmap is a comprehensive national strategy to accelerate the development and commercialization of fusion energy by the mid-2030s.
[Source 13: DOE Fusion Science and Technology Roadmap] DOE source on the Fusion Science and Technology Roadmap and mid-2030s commercialization ambition.

[Source 3: DOE Fusion Innovation Research Engine selectees] Department of Energy (DOE) today announced $107 million in funding for six projects in the Fusion Innovative Research Engine (FIRE) Collaboratives , and that several privately funded fusion companies have completed early critical-path science and technology (S&T) milestones in the Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program (“the Milestone Program”).

[Source 3: DOE Fusion Innovation Research Engine selectees] The DOE Milestone-Based Fusion Development Program was first authorized in the Energy Act of 2020 and received its first funding appropriation in fiscal year 2022.

[Source 14: DOE FY 2027 Fusion Energy Sciences budget request] Science/Fusion Energy Sciences FY 2027 Congressional Justification Fusion Energy Sciences Overview The Fusion Energy Sciences (FES) program is advancing in alignment with a set of national priorities that emphasize energy security, economic competitiveness, and U.

[Source 14: DOE FY 2027 Fusion Energy Sciences budget request] It’s six Core Challenge Areas are strongly aligned to the 2020 Fusion Energy Sciences Advisory Committee (FESAC) Long-Range Plan (LRP): structural materials, plasma-facing components and plasma-material interactions, confinement approaches, the fuel cycle, blankets, and fusion plant engineering and system integration.

[Source 14: DOE FY 2027 Fusion Energy Sciences budget request]  Closing the Fusion Cycle: Initiate design of an Integrated Blanket and Fuel Cycle Test Facility (IB-FCTF), consistent with recent FESAC reports and following design and cost estimate work in FY 2026.

[Source 14: DOE FY 2027 Fusion Energy Sciences budget request] The 131 Science/Fusion Energy Sciences FY 2027 Congressional Justification approach to delivering an IB-FCTF will be a phased approach that delivers component capabilities integrated over time, to support closing the critical S&T gaps identified in the Roadmap blanket and fuel cycl

[Source 14: DOE FY 2027 Fusion Energy Sciences budget request] With continued funding for the Established Program to Stimulate Competitive Research (EPSCoR), FES builds strategic programs to enhance SC-sponsored fusion-relevant research in key states and territories.

[Source 1: U.S. DOE Fusion Energy] DOE’s ability to support this Roadmap’s milestones and timelines of scaling up the domestic fusion private sector by the 2030s is contingent on the development of future public private partnerships.
[Source 2: U.S. DOE Fusion Energy Sciences] The Department of Energy has been a leader in fusion energy research since the 1950s and supports groundbreaking advances today.
[Source 3: DOE Fusion Innovation Research Engine selectees] The program is authorized for a total of $415 million through fiscal year 2027 in the CHIPS and Science Act of 2022.

[Source 13: DOE Fusion Science and Technology Roadmap] The Roadmap defines DOE’s Build–Innovate–Grow strategy to align public investment and private innovation to deliver commercial fusion power to the grid by the mid-2030s.

[Source 13: DOE Fusion Science and Technology Roadmap] DOE’s ability to support this Roadmap’s milestones and timelines of scaling up the domestic fusion private sector by the 2030s is contingent on the development of future public private partnerships.

[Source 3: DOE Fusion Innovation Research Engine selectees] To date, Milestone awardees have collectively raised over $350 million of new private funding since their selection into the program was announced in May 2023, compared to the $46 million of federal funding initially committed for negotiated milestones.
